intro: Trichoglottis dawsoniana, also known as the Dawson's orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The Dawson's orchid has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Dawson's orchid include tea infusions and extracts, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
